title: Antipose XII

{{Quote|Tell them the secret word is '<nowiki />''dubesor''<nowiki />'—I understand that's a native insult on Antipose XII.|Lando Calrissian, to Vuffi Raa|<ref name="LCFO">''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]''</ref>}}
'''Antipose XII''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. It had [[Color/Legends|violet]] [[Atmosphere/Legends|skies]], and its native inhabitants used the word "dubesor" as an insult. The pleasure [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]] ''[[Arleen]]'' made [[Standard year/Legends|year]]-long tours from the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]] to the celestial body [[Antipose IX]] as well as Antipose XII. When the [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]] and [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] at one point used "dubesor" as a pre-designated password, it made him wonder what the natives of Antipose XII were doing that [[Standard day/Legends|night]].

==Description==
Antipose XII was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that occupied the twelfth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="CSWE Antipose" /> around the [[Antipose system star|star]] of<ref name="Antipose star" /> the [[Antipose system/Legends|Antipose system]],<ref name="CSWE Antipose" /> a part of the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Along with the rest of the [[Star system/Legends|system]], Antipose XII was connected by the [[Falko Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] to the [[Falko system/Legends|Falko]] and [[Paulking system/Legends|Paulking systems]].<ref name="TEA" /> The planet's [[Atmosphere/Legends|skies]] were described as the "most violet skies in the universe."<ref name="Gamer5">{{GamerCite|5|A Campaign Guide to the Centrality}}</ref>

==History==
[[File:Lando-FlamewindCover.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Lando Calrissian was instructed to use the Antipose XII insult "dubesor" as a password.]]
The pleasure [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]] ''[[Arleen]]'',<ref name="Gamer5" /> constructed<ref name="LCMS">''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]''</ref> by [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of [[The Lando Calrissian Adventures (omnibus)|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] novel trilogy, of which ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'' and ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'' are the first and second installments, respectively, between [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]].</ref> made [[Standard year/Legends|year]]-long tours from the Oseon system to the celestial body [[Antipose IX]] as well as Antipose XII.<ref name="Gamer5" /> At some point between 3 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> the [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]] and [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] was forced to travel to the heavily defended [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] [[Oseon 5792]], which served as the private estate of the trillionaire [[Bohhuah Mutdah/Legends|Bohhuah Mutdah]]. Calrissian was given a secret password—"dubesor," an insult used by the inhabitants of Antipose XII—for approaching the asteroid, and at some point he learned of the context behind the word. The captain relayed that information to his [[Pilot/Legends|copilot]], the [[Class Two multi-phasic robot|droid]] [[Vuffi Raa]], as the latter was communicating the password to a [[Picket ship/Legends|picket ship]] that had intercepted them in Oseon 5792's vicinity.<ref name="LCFO" />

After the pair were given instructions for landing their [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|light freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], on the asteroid, Calrissian wondered whether the natives of Antipose XII were loudly celebrating in a local [[Cantina/Legends|saloon]] that [[Standard day/Legends|night]] and calling each other "dubesor." The gambler grimly noted to himself that such activity would be vastly preferable to his upcoming mission, which involved [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] two [[Police officer/Legends|law enforcement officers]] onto Oseon 5792 and helping them arrest Mutdah on charges related to the trafficking and use of illegal [[Spice/Legends|narcotics]].<ref name="LCFO" />

==Inhabitants==
Constituting a sparse population,<ref name="Gamer5" /> the inhabitants of Antipose XII used the native slang insult "dubesor" in their [[Language/Legends|lexicon]].<ref name="CSWE Antipose" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Flamewind_of_Oseon_Cover.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Antipose XII was introduced in ''Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon''.]]
Antipose XII was first mentioned in ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'', the [[1983]] second entry in [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] trilogy of novels by [[L. Neil Smith]].<ref name="LCFO" /> "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, placed the Antipose system, and therefore Antipose XII, in the [[Seswenna sector/Legends|Seswenna sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> In addition, "Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are" misspelled the name of Antipose XII as "Antipose XI."<ref name="Twinkle" />

''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', released in [[2008]], also created an apparent contradiction by stating that the Antipose system, including Antipose XII, was situated "not far from Centrality,"<ref name="CSWE Antipose" /> despite the [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article "[[A Campaign Guide to the Centrality]]"—authored by [[Michael Kogge]] and published in the [[Star Wars Gamer 5|fifth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="Gamer5" /> on [[July 24]], [[2001]]<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20010627.html|text=''Gamer'' #5. A Domain of Evil it is.}}</ref>—previously establishing that the system indeed constituted a part of the sector.<ref name="Gamer5" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently overrode both the ''Voyages SF'' 13 placement and the information from ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' by placing the Antipose system in grid square U-8 as part of the Centrality.<ref name="TEA" />
